symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral facial pain and pressure over cheeks and forehead, worse on bending forward

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Positional facial pressure over the sinuses is a cardinal feature of rhinosinusitis.
Constant positional pressure is unlike the sharp electric paroxysms of trigeminal neuralgia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Thick, discoloured (yellow-green) purulent nasal discharge developing over 48 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Purulent-appearing discharge occurs in uncomplicated viral rhinosinusitis; colour does not reliably distinguish viral from bacterial within 10 days.
Discoloured discharge is often over-interpreted as bacterial; alone it is a weak, non-specific pointer.

symptoms.symptom_name: Coryzal prodrome with symptom duration under 10 days and a steady (non-biphasic) course

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Onset from a viral cold, duration <10 days and no double sickening strongly favour a viral, self-limiting course.
Bacterial rhinosinusitis is favoured by >10 days without improvement or a biphasic worsening — neither present.

symptoms.symptom_name: Upper maxillary tooth ache and reduced sense of smell (hyposmia)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Referred maxillary dental pain and hyposmia are recognised features of maxillary sinus involvement.
Diffuse maxillary ache across the sinus rather than a single tooth argues against a focal dental abscess.
Nocturnal post-nasal-drip cough is a common, non-specific accompaniment of upper-airway inflammation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of orbital signs — no periorbital swelling/redness, diplopia or painful eye movements

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative orbital screen makes orbital cellulitis unlikely, but telehealth cannot fully examine the eye, so it remains a safety-net trigger.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of intracranial signs — no severe unremitting headache, neck stiffness, photophobia or confusion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative intracranial screen makes central spread unlikely; retained as a mandatory safety-net trigger.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sinus tenderness on palpation/percussion and purulent discharge in the middle meatus on anterior rhinoscopy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-measured examination findings that support sinus involvement; sealed — the AI Doctor infers from history and video, not from palpation or rhinoscopy it cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Low-grade temperature 37.6C and stable observations (BP 132/86, HR 94, RR 16, SpO2 98%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Only a low-grade fever with otherwise stable vitals argues against a high-fever bacterial or systemic complication; measured vitals are clinician-grade and sealed (only a patient-reported home thermometer reading reaches the AI Doctor).
